2010-08-01.title: Robert C. Tucker, a Scholar of Marx, Stalin and Soviet Affairs, Dies at 92
2010-08-01.abstract: Mr. Tucker’s frustrations in persuading the authorities in Stalin’s Russia to let his new Russian wife accompany him home to the United States gave him crucial  insights into the Soviet leader.

2010-08-01.title: Erich Steidtmann, Ex-Nazi Officer in Inquiry, Dies at 95
2010-08-01.abstract: Mr. Steidtmann died three months after German prosecutors reopened an investigation into whether he had participated in massacres of thousands of Polish Jews.

2010-08-01.title: Building the Future With the Past
2010-08-01.abstract: Steven Erlanger, The Times's Paris bureau chief, visited Burgundy, where artisans are using 13th-century techniques to build a replica of a medieval chateau.

2010-08-01.title: Flooding in Pakistan
2010-08-01.abstract: Monsoon-driven floods are reported to have killed at least 800 people and destroyed thousands of homes in the past week, officials said Saturday, as some of the 1 million Pakistanis affected were reported with fevers, diarrhea and other waterborne illnesses.

2010-08-01.title: Targeted Killing Is New U.S. Focus in Afghanistan
2010-08-01.abstract: The Obama administration is starting to count more heavily on hunting down insurgents and less on winning over civilians.

2010-08-02.title: In Koreas, Floods Carry Land Mines
2010-08-02.abstract: People along the rivers and beaches near the Korean border were confronted by a deadly surprise  as dozens of mines from the North washed up in flooding.

2010-08-02.title: Protests Test Patience of Mexico City Drivers
2010-08-02.abstract: Since Mexico City does not regulate protests, people are free to block traffic whenever they please.

2010-08-02.title: Immigrant Maids Flee Lives of Abuse in Kuwait
2010-08-02.abstract: Hundreds of maids who fled from their employers now sleep in improvised shelters in their countries’ embassies.

2010-08-02.title: Iraq's Dark Age
2010-08-02.abstract: Eight hot summers after American troops entered Iraq, one of the most basic needs, electricity, remains unreliable.

2010-08-02.title: A Benchmark of Progress, Electrical Grid Fails Iraqis
2010-08-02.abstract: As the American combat mission officially ends this month, Iraq’s government is still struggling to provide one of the most basic services, generating disillusionment and dissent.

2010-08-03.title: Kashmir: More Deadly Protests Over Disputed Region
2010-08-03.abstract: The toll of a deadly summer of angry protest in the  Kashmir region rose to at least 33 people after a bloody weekend in which security forces fired on stone-throwing protesters, officials said.

2010-08-03.title: China: 11 Die in Attack by Man Driving Shovel Loader
2010-08-03.abstract: An enraged man drove a shovel loader through shops and over cars at a coal depot in northeast China on Sunday, killing 11 people and injuring 17 more, officials said.

2010-08-03.title: Nepal: Third Failure in Parliament to Elect a Premier
2010-08-03.abstract: A large number of lawmakers remained neutral and neither of the two runoff candidates got a majority of votes.

2010-08-03.title: China: Mao’s Grandson Gets Army Promotion
2010-08-03.abstract: Mao Xinyu, 14, has been elevated to major general from senior colonel in the People’s Liberation Army, making him one of the youngest ever to achieve that rank.

2010-08-03.title: China: Police Break Up Protest After Hundreds Support TV Network’s Use of Cantonese
2010-08-03.abstract: Hundreds of people in Guangzhou had gathered to demonstrate against a proposal by politicians to change the programming language on a local television network.

2010-08-04.title: Building a New History by Exhuming Bolívar
2010-08-04.abstract: Venezuelans are scratching their heads over President Hugo Chávez’s motives for the removal of Simon Bolívar’s remains.

2010-08-04.title: South Africa: Ex-police Chief Is Sentenced to 15 Years
2010-08-04.abstract: Jackie Selebi was sentenced to 15 years in prison on Tuesday after his conviction last month for accepting bribes from a drug kingpin.

2010-08-04.title: Mexico: Death Toll From Drug-Related Violence Is Thousands Higher Than Was Reported Earlier
2010-08-04.abstract: The director of Mexico’s intelligence agency put the new toll at a “little more than 28,000 murders” since President Felipe Calderón took office in December 2006.

2010-08-04.title: After Haiti Quake, the Chaos of U.S. Adoptions
2010-08-04.abstract: Washington responded to the disaster in Haiti by lifting visa requirements, and some safeguards were ignored.

2010-08-04.title: 4 Believed Dead in China School Attack
2010-08-04.abstract: Three children and at least one teacher were reported on Wednesday to have died in a Tuesday afternoon knife attack at a kindergarten in eastern China.

2010-08-05.title: Framing the Afghan Debate With a Magazine Cover
2010-08-05.abstract: A portrait of one girl’s trauma ignites a larger debate about the war in Afghanistan and the fate of women in Afghanistan after U.S. troops leave.

2010-08-05.title: Violence Said to Be Rising in Sudan’s Darfur Region
2010-08-05.abstract: Calling the situation a “bleak picture,” the head of United Nations peacekeeping told a news conference that security had deteriorated significantly as optimism for a cease-fire in 2009 faded.

2010-08-05.title: As It Shrinks, the Dead Sea Nourishes Promises of an Economic Bloom
2010-08-05.abstract: The Dead Sea may be shrinking, but the governments of Israel, Jordan, and the Palestinian Authority see opportunities for cooperation and growth there.

2010-08-05.title: Wyclef Jean Confirms Plans to Run for Haitian Presidency
2010-08-05.abstract: The Haitian-American hip-hop star said that he felt compelled by the urgency of his country’s situation to interrupt his life as a musician and make a bid for the presidency.

2010-08-05.title: Lawyers Win Right to Aid U.S. Target
2010-08-05.abstract: A radical Muslim cleric who is a United States citizen has been designated for killing without trial.

2010-08-06.title: Wildfires Spread in Russia
2010-08-06.abstract: Russia’s worst heat wave since record-keeping began here more than 130 years ago has exacerbated a severe drought and wildfires, which have been spreading across the country’s central and western regions.

2010-08-06.title: In Indonesia, Many Eyes Follow Money for Hajj
2010-08-06.abstract: Millions are on a government waiting list to go to Mecca, but groups say that officials and politicians misuse their deposits.

2010-08-06.title: South Korea: Naval Exercises Start
2010-08-06.abstract: South Korea on Thursday began one of its largest naval exercises ever in waters near a disputed border with North Korea, despite the North’s threat to retaliate.

2010-08-06.title: Mexico: Marriage Law Upheld
2010-08-06.abstract: The Supreme Court upheld Mexico City’s same-sex marriage law on Thursday in an 8-to-2 decision, but postponed deciding related issues, like the adoption of children by same-sex couples.

2010-08-06.title: Pakistan: U.S. Flood Aid Arrives
2010-08-06.abstract: American helicopters began evacuating people and delivering aid to flood-ravaged northwestern Pakistan on Thursday in an effort to aid Pakistani rescue efforts and sway hearts and minds.

2010-08-07.title: Navy Building’s Next Tenant Has Paris Guessing
2010-08-07.abstract: The Hôtel de la Marine, the building where Marie Antoinette’s death certificate was signed, and from whose balcony her execution by guillotine was witnessed, may soon be up for grabs.

2010-08-07.title: First Lady’s Spain Vacation Draws Criticism
2010-08-07.abstract: The image of Michelle Obama vacationing abroad while Americans struggle back home struck some as ill-timed.

2010-08-07.title: China: Crackdown on ‘Vulgarity and Indecency’ Is Expected
2010-08-07.abstract: Chinese officials are expected to start a crackdown soon on “vulgarity and indecency” in the media, including television dating shows, according to a report Friday in the English-language edition of the official newspaper Global Times.

2010-08-07.title: Mexico: 14 Die in Prison Fighting
2010-08-07.abstract: A fight between two groups of inmates at the Santa Adelaida prison in the border city of Matamoros on Friday killed at least 14 prisoners, who attacked one another with homemade knives and sharpened objects, Tamaulipas state officials said.

2010-08-07.title: Former Mandela Fund Official Says Model Gave Him Diamonds
2010-08-07.abstract: Jeremy Ractliffe confirmed that he received “three small uncut diamonds” from the supermodel Naomi Campbell the day after she attended a 1997 dinner given by Nelson Mandela and attended by Charles Taylor, then the president of Liberia.

2010-08-08.title: Flooding Continues in Pakistan
2010-08-08.abstract: More rain soaked Pakistan, and even heavier downpours were forecast for coming days.

2010-08-08.title: North Korea Seizes South Korean Boat Near Border
2010-08-08.abstract: North Korea seized a fishing boat in waters near the eastern sea border, the South Korean Coast Guard said.

2010-08-08.title: Landslides Kill 127 in China
2010-08-08.abstract: A landslide buried homes in a remote mountainous region of China’s Gansu Province, leaving 127 people dead.

2010-08-08.title: India Asks, Should Food Be a Right for the Poor?
2010-08-08.abstract: India is debating whether to dismantle an inefficient, decades-old food distribution system and try something radical.

2010-08-09.title: Iraq’s Excuser-in-Chief Speaks Up
2010-08-09.abstract: Who, all of a sudden, has piped up from his Baghdad prison cell and urged President Obama not to proceed with American troop withdrawal schedule? None other than Tariq Aziz, an incongruous figure as the chief apologist for one of modern history’s most murderous regimes.

2010-08-09.title: U.S. Victims Identified as Bodies Arrive in Kabul
2010-08-09.abstract: F.B.I. agents confirmed that 10 bodies were all members of an American-based group.

2010-08-09.title: Utopian Dream Becomes Battleground in France
2010-08-09.abstract: A community built in the 1970s slowly degraded before it finally burst into flames.

2010-08-09.title: India Swoons Over Its Chess Champ, and Even the Game
2010-08-09.abstract: Viswanathan Anand, who has held the world title for three years, appears to have earned the fame that India usually reserves for movie stars, cricket players and politicians.

2010-08-09.title: Doubts Rise in Rwanda as Election Is Held
2010-08-09.abstract: The question is not whether President Paul Kagame will be re-elected but how broad his support is.

2010-08-10.title: Secretary General Defends Appointment of Corruption Official
2010-08-10.abstract: Ban Ki-moon said that accusations that he had sought to subvert the independence of corruption investigations at the organization by appointing the body’s senior investigator were “unfair.”

2010-08-10.title: China Seizes on a Dark Chapter for Tibet
2010-08-10.abstract: The government has appropriated a British invasion in 1904 as part of the long history of imperialist efforts to dismantle China.

2010-08-10.title: Portugal Gives Itself a Clean-Energy Makeover
2010-08-10.abstract: The country’s experience in converting to renewable power shows rapid progress is achievable, but also highlights the price.

2010-08-10.title: Petraeus Scheduled to Start Spate of Media Interviews
2010-08-10.abstract: Gen. David H. Petraeus will appear on NBC’s “Meet the Press,” the “CBS Evening News” and ABC’s “Good Morning America.”

2010-08-10.title: U.N. Chief Recommends Small Steps on Climate
2010-08-10.abstract: Secretary General Ban Ki-moon said he doubted that member states would reach a new worldwide climate change agreement in December at a conference in Mexico.

2010-08-11.title: Dressing Artists’ Hub in Something Button-Down
2010-08-11.abstract: The threatened eviction of artists to make way for a lavish new development illustrates the divide between Berlin’s unique past and its status as the capital of a stable, reunified country.

2010-08-11.title: Leaders Repair Colombia-Venezuela Ties
2010-08-11.abstract: The leaders of Colombia and Venezuela met in an effort to repair relations that deteriorated over claims that Colombian guerrillas were operating from Venezuela.

2010-08-11.title: Scottish Government Pressed to Release Medical Advice That Led to Lockerbie Bomber’s Release
2010-08-11.abstract: The government came under fresh pressure at home to justify its decision to release the bomber, Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi, on medical grounds.

2010-08-11.title: Turkey: Adviser Could Face Polygamy Charges
2010-08-11.abstract: An adviser to the prime minister’s office could face up to two years in prison if he is charged with polygamy after describing his simultaneous relationship with three women in a book.

2010-08-11.title: Rwanda: Voting Irregularities Seen in Lopsided Victory
2010-08-11.abstract: President Paul Kagame’s landslide victory in Rwanda’s presidential election Monday was “largely accurate,” despite shortcomings and irregularities, electoral observers said Tuesday.

2010-08-12.title: Chinese Hospitals Are Battlegrounds of Discontent
2010-08-12.abstract: In 2006, patients or their relatives attacked more than 5,500 medical workers, reflecting wide discontent with China’s public health care system.

2010-08-12.title: U.S. Provides Additional Helicopters to the Relief Effort
2010-08-12.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said Wednesday that the United States would triple the number of military helicopters deployed to assist Pakistan in flood relief.

2010-08-12.title: Unrest Is Undermining Hopes for Afghan Vote
2010-08-12.abstract: President Hamid Karzai’s ability to deliver a credible parliamentary election in September is in doubt.

2010-08-12.title: Tibetan Writer’s Intellectual Journey Leads to Trial
2010-08-12.abstract: Tragyal, who writes under the name Shogdung, has advocated civil disobedience against the government; his arrest came amid a two-year crackdown by authorities.

2010-08-12.title: New Flood Warnings Raise Fears in Pakistan
2010-08-12.abstract: Pakistan issued new warnings of floods that could last into the weekend as authorities and aid groups scrambled to confront the toll from a growing humanitarian disaster.

2010-08-13.title: Ambassador Leaves Iraq With Much Still Unsettled
2010-08-13.abstract: Christopher R. Hill is leaving as ambassador with an American military withdrawal under way but a big task incomplete: the formation of a government.

2010-08-13.title: Diving for Pearls, and a Connection to the Past
2010-08-13.abstract: An annual expedition allows young Kuwaiti men to celebrate the nation’s largely forgotten seafaring past.

2010-08-13.title: In Kabul, a Service for Slain Aid Workers
2010-08-13.abstract: If there was a theme to the memorial service, which was self-consciously non-religious, it was the thought that these were people who died doing something they believed in passionately, and the best way to honor them was to keep at it.

2010-08-13.title: Canada Stops Ship Carrying 490 People
2010-08-13.abstract: Canada took a cargo ship carrying nearly 500 people thought to be Tamil migrants who had fled Sri Lanka to try to to enter Canada as refugees.

2010-08-13.title: Iran Shows What It Says Is Murder Confession
2010-08-13.abstract: State television broadcast what it portrayed as a murder confession by a woman who had been sentenced to death by stoning on adultery charges.

2010-08-14.title: Sri Lanka Convicts Ex-General
2010-08-14.abstract: The runner-up in Sri Lanka’s last presidential election, Gen. Sarath Fonseka, was convicted in a court-martial of politicking while still in uniform and sentenced to a dishonorable discharge.

2010-08-14.title: U.S. Sees North Korea as Rattling Sabers for Heir
2010-08-14.abstract: Kim Jong-un, the son of North Korea’s leader, is thought to have been the force behind a new wave of aggressive actions and appears to be in line to succeed his father.

2010-08-14.title: Russia: Official Says Wildfires Have Not Raised Radiation Levels
2010-08-14.abstract: Wildfires in territories contaminated by the 1986 Chernobyl nuclear disaster have not led to a rise in radiation levels in Russia, the head of Russia’s nuclear energy agency said Friday.

2010-08-14.title: Somalia: 5 Soldiers in Puntland Are Killed in Battle With Militants
2010-08-14.abstract: In a radio interview, an insurgent warlord, Sheik Mohamed Said Atom, claimed credit for the killings and vowed to “continue our fight until we overthrow the Puntland administration.”

2010-08-14.title: Cuba: Articles by Castro Laud Ex-Candidate From Mexico
2010-08-14.abstract: Comments by Fidel Castro in support of the runner-up in Mexico’s 2006 presidential election, Andrés Manuel López Obrador, threaten to revive diplomatic tensions between Cuba and Mexico.

2010-08-15.title: A Burial Follows a Fight in Afghanistan
2010-08-15.abstract: The dispute between ethnic Hazaras and Afghan nomads is centuries old.

2010-08-15.title: In Weather Chaos, a Case for Global Warming
2010-08-15.abstract: Far-flung flooding, drought and heat are reviving the question of whether global warming is to blame.

2010-08-15.title: South Korean Leader Proposes a Tax to Finance Reunification
2010-08-15.abstract: President Lee Myung-bak’s proposed tax would help fund the cost of reuniting with North Korea.

2010-08-15.title: Petraeus Opposes a Rapid Pullout in Afghanistan
2010-08-15.abstract: The commander of American and NATO forces said he did not come to Afghanistan to preside over a “graceful exit.”

2010-08-15.title: Flooding in Pakistan Spreads
2010-08-15.abstract: Flooding that began more than two weeks ago in the northwest now affects about one-quarter of Pakistan.

2010-08-16.title: Workers Let Go by China’s Banks Are Putting Up a Fight
2010-08-16.abstract: Many employees who lost their jobs as state-run banks restructured are organizing to demand new jobs or compensation, but they face a daunting task.

2010-08-16.title: Japan’s Cabinet Shuns Shrine on Anniversary of War’s End
2010-08-16.abstract: While no serving Japanese prime minister has visited Yasukuni Shrine in four years, Prime Minister Naoto Kan distanced himself even further by announcing that no one in his government would visit.

2010-08-16.title: Bypassing Resistance, Brazil Prepares to Build a Dam
2010-08-16.abstract: Despite persistent opposition by environmental and indigenous groups, initial construction on the Belo Monte dam, which will be the third-largest in the world, is slated to begin next month.

2010-08-16.title: Soviet Legacy Lingers as Estonia Defines Its People
2010-08-16.abstract: A hundred thousand people in Estonia, most of them ethnic Russians born and raised in Estonia, are not legal Estonians, as if they are refugees in their own homeland.

2010-08-16.title: Chinese Health Ministry Clears Milk Powder in Latest Scare
2010-08-16.abstract: China’s health ministry announced that it had found no clinical evidence that milk powder had been responsible for apparent breast development in three infant girls in south-central China.

2010-08-17.title: High in the Andes, Keeping an Incan Mystery Alive
2010-08-17.abstract: A town in the Andes has one of the last known collections of khipus — weavings that may explain how the Incas ruled without a written language — still in ritual use.

2010-08-17.title: U.S. Said to Plan Easing Rules for Travel to Cuba
2010-08-17.abstract: Officials said an embargo against the Castro government would continue even as travel opportunities expanded.

2010-08-17.title: Star’s Candidacy in Haiti Puts Focus on Charity
2010-08-17.abstract: How Wyclef Jean, the hip-hop star, has guided his charity in Haiti offers one barometer of his ability to lead.

2010-08-17.title: Mysteries Woven Into Peru's Past
2010-08-17.abstract: In a town high in the Peruvian Andes, the Quechua people guard the mysterious records of their past: ancient weavings called khipus.

2010-08-17.title: Gaza: Fatal Cross-Border Clash
2010-08-17.abstract: A Palestinian militant was killed and an Israeli soldier was lightly wounded Monday in cross-border clashes in the southeast Gaza Strip, the Israeli military said.

2010-08-18.title: U.N. Sounds Alarm on Aid for Pakistan
2010-08-18.abstract: The plight of flood victims has not yet prompted donors to open their hearts and wallets, according to relief agencies.

2010-08-18.title: A Dutch City Seeks to End Drug Tourism
2010-08-18.abstract: Struggling to reduce traffic and crime, Maastricht is pushing to make its legalized use of recreational drugs a Dutch-only policy.

2010-08-18.title: With a Sit-Down Stand, a Briton Kicks the Boot
2010-08-18.abstract: One man’s defiance of a towing company has led to a government proposal to change the law on “clamping” of cars.

2010-08-18.title: Lawmakers in Mexico to Debate Drug Fight
2010-08-18.abstract: With the pace of killings rising, President Felipe Calderón set up high-level forums in an effort to show that the Mexican government was willing to engage its critics and listen to suggestions.

2010-08-18.title: Blackwater Founder Moves to Abu Dhabi, Records Say
2010-08-18.abstract: Erik Prince, whose company made millions on government contracts in Iraq, is said to “need a break from America.”

2010-08-19.title: Civilians to Take U.S. Lead as Military Leaves Iraq
2010-08-19.abstract: As the U.S. military prepares to leave Iraq, the Obama administration is planning a remarkable civilian effort.

2010-08-19.title: Scheme Rattles Benin, an Anchor of Stability
2010-08-19.abstract: A Ponzi scheme has shaken the economy in a nation that has long been an exemplar of stability in West Africa.

2010-08-19.title: In Afghanistan, More Attacks on Officials and a Protest Over a Deadly NATO Raid
2010-08-19.abstract: Police officers and a provincial official died in separate attacks in southern Afghanistan, while NATO disputed accounts that its forces killed two civilians in a night raid.

2010-08-19.title: Protests in Kashmir Test India’s Inclusive Ideals
2010-08-19.abstract: Kashmir is a blood-soaked exception to India’s historic embrace of many ethnic, linguistic and religious groups.

2010-08-19.title: U.S. Strategy in Pakistan Is Upended by Floods
2010-08-19.abstract: The United States is confronting a vast humanitarian crisis and militant groups aiming to exploit the misery.

2010-08-20.title: Australia’s Labor Party Faces a Fight for Survival
2010-08-20.abstract: While the country emerged unscathed from the downturn, the governing Labor Party faces a political battle over immigration and spending.

2010-08-20.title: Moscow Weather Back to Normal After Fires
2010-08-20.abstract: After weeks of gagging on noxious fumes from wildfires, Muscovites are finally in the clear.

2010-08-20.title: New Afghan Intelligence Chief Aims to Build Trust
2010-08-20.abstract: Rahmatullah Nabil said he wanted to demonstrate that Afghans were capable of taking charge of their own security.

2010-08-20.title: U.S. Assures Israel That Iran Threat Is Not Imminent
2010-08-20.abstract: The Obama administration has persuaded Israel that it would take roughly a year for Iran to complete a nuclear weapon, according to American officials.

2010-08-20.title: In Iraq War, Soldiers Say They Had a Job to Do
2010-08-20.abstract: Even with the end of combat operations, Iraq remains a job that inspires emotion only among those who do it.

2010-08-21.title: Blackwater Reaches Deal on U.S. Export Violations
2010-08-21.abstract: The $42 million fine allows the controversial security company, now called Xe Services, to avoid criminal charges while being able to obtain government contracts.

2010-08-21.title: U.S. Anti-Islam Protest Seen as Lift for Extremists
2010-08-21.abstract: Some counterterrorism experts said that the sentiment against plans to build an Islamic center near ground zero is playing into the hands of extremists.

2010-08-21.title: In Mideast Talks, Scant Hopes From the Beginning
2010-08-21.abstract: There is little confidence that the Obama administration’s goal of reaching a Mideast peace deal in one year can be met.

2010-08-21.title: Mexico: 6 Police Officers Held in Fatal Abduction of Mayor
2010-08-21.abstract: Five local police officers and a transit officer were arrested in connection with the kidnapping and killing of Mayor Edelmiro Cavazos of Santiago.

2010-08-21.title: Russia: Putin Ousts Forestry Chief
2010-08-21.abstract: The prime minister fired Aleksei Savinov in the wake of this summer’s horrific wildfires, which consumed more than two million acres of land and caused more than 50 deaths.

2010-08-22.title: India Tries Using Cash Bonuses to Slow Birthrates
2010-08-22.abstract: As its population threatens to turn from an asset into a burden, India is looking for ways to encourage delayed childbirth.

2010-08-22.title: After Close Australia Vote, Parties Seek Coalition
2010-08-22.abstract: The two main candidates in Australia’s cliffhanger election began scrambling Sunday to win the support of three independent lawmakers and a newly elected Greens party representative.

2010-08-22.title: Sweden Defends Reversal of Warrant for Founder of WikiLeaks
2010-08-22.abstract: Swedish prosecutors said that they had made no mistakes in issuing an arrest warrant for Julian Assange, the founder of WikiLeaks, and withdrawing it hours later.

2010-08-22.title: Severe Flooding Hits Northeast China
2010-08-22.abstract: More than 127,000 people were evacuated in the northeast after torrential rains.

2010-08-22.title: Relentless Rain Batters China
2010-08-22.abstract: Rescue teams are battling raging flood waters in China's southwest Sichuan province, and torrential rains in the northeastern Liaoning province caused a river bordering North Korea to break its banks.

2010-08-23.title: Gaza Mall Seeks to Make Statement of Resolve
2010-08-23.abstract: A new shopping center has deep symbolic value for Hamas and the group of local investors behind it, but has drawn criticism from Israel’s fiercest defenders.

2010-08-23.title: Proposed Restrictions on the News Media Cause Alarm in South Africa
2010-08-23.abstract: The Protection of Information Bill and a plan for a media tribunal have come amid increasing hostility between the governing African National Congress and the press.

2010-08-23.title: At Least 150 Women Raped in Weekend Raid in Congo
2010-08-23.abstract: Officials said that the attack was unusual because of the large number of victims and the fact that they were raped by more than one attacker simultaneously.

2010-08-23.title: Pakistanis Tell of Motive in Taliban Leader’s Arrest
2010-08-23.abstract: Pakistani officials say they set out to capture Abdul Ghani Baradar because they wanted to shut down secret peace talks he had been conducting with the Afghan government.

2010-08-23.title: Fatality in Iraq Is First After Deadline
2010-08-23.abstract: An American soldier was killed on Sunday, marking the first American fatality since the military declared last week that the last combat unit had pulled out of the country.

2010-08-24.title: Plotting Doubted in WikiLeaks Case
2010-08-24.abstract: Those who have knowledge of Swedish prosecutors’ sexual abuse accusations against Julian Assange discount conspiracy theories linking it to efforts to discredit WikiLeaks.

2010-08-24.title: Iran: Officials Suspended Over Detainees’ Treatment
2010-08-24.abstract: Three judiciary officials were suspended in connection with the mistreatment of detainees at a notorious holding center during the unrest that followed last year’s disputed presidential election.

2010-08-24.title: In the Fields of Italy, a Conflict Over Corn
2010-08-24.abstract: An agronomist, defying the government, has planted genetically modified corn. Environmentalists have also taken matters into their own hands.

2010-08-24.title: Afghans Say NATO Troops Killed 8 Civilians in Raid
2010-08-24.abstract: Officials and residents of Baghlan Province in northern Afghanistan said the civilians were killed during an early morning raid.

2010-08-24.title: In Scarred Land, a Haven for Victims of Acid Burns
2010-08-24.abstract: Cambodia has a history of acid attacks — a rare and extreme form of revenge or punishment.

2010-08-25.title: To Catch Cairo Overflow, 2 Megacities Rise in Sand
2010-08-25.abstract: The Egyptian government is building two megacities outside Cairo from scratch.

2010-08-25.title: Britain: Daughter Is Born to Prime Minister and Wife
2010-08-25.abstract: Prime Minister David Cameron’s wife, Samantha Cameron, gave birth to the couple’s fourth child, a daughter, on Tuesday.

2010-08-25.title: Israel: Arabic to Be Taught to Fifth-Graders
2010-08-25.abstract: The study of Arabic will be compulsory for fifth-grade students in Jewish public schools under a pilot project starting in September.

2010-08-25.title: France: Rights Group Faults Roma Policy
2010-08-25.abstract: The European Commission against Racism and Intolerance on Tuesday joined critics of France’s efforts to deport Roma who overstay their visas and to demolish illegal Roma camps.

2010-08-25.title: A Relieved Chile Braces for a Long Mine Rescue
2010-08-25.abstract: Trapped miners in Chile have not heard the news that it could take months to pull them to the surface.

2010-08-26.title: Dispute in the Negev
2010-08-26.abstract: Bedouins, members of Israel's Arab minority who are settled in the Negev Desert in southern Israel, are fighting government plans to relocate them from their traditional homes.

2010-08-26.title: A Test of Wills Over a Patch of Desert
2010-08-26.abstract: A tug of war between Israeli bulldozers and Bedouins who rebuild has turned into a symbol of a larger land dispute.

2010-08-26.title: U.N. Knew of Rebels in Area of Congo Rapes
2010-08-26.abstract: The United Nations sent an e-mail alerting staff members that Rwandan rebels were in the area, but it did not mention rape; at least 179 women were attacked.

2010-08-26.title: Looking at Islamic Center Debate, World Sees U.S.
2010-08-26.abstract: For some around the world, the dispute over an Islamic center near ground zero is a confirmation of their views.

2010-08-26.title: Karzai Aide in Corruption Inquiry Is Tied to C.I.A.
2010-08-26.abstract: Officials say an aide to the president of Afghanistan being investigated for corruption is being paid by the C.I.A.

2010-08-27.title: Germany: Singer Is Convicted for Transmitting H.I.V.
2010-08-27.abstract: Nadja Benaissa, who performed with the band No Angels, received a two-year suspended sentence because she expressed deep remorse throughout the trial.

2010-08-27.title: Facing Long Mine Rescue, Chile Spares No Expense
2010-08-27.abstract: President Sebastián Piñera has staked his nascent presidency on rescuing the miners, sparing no attempted innovation to maintain their psychological health.

2010-08-27.title: Pakistan Flood Sets Back Infrastructure by Years
2010-08-27.abstract: The destruction of roads, schools and health clinics could further weaken the civilian administration.

2010-08-27.title: Pakistan’s Progress Is Lost in Floodwaters
2010-08-27.abstract: Pakistani and international relief officials despaired that the nation’s worst natural calamity had ruined just about every physical strand that knit the country together.

2010-08-27.title: Assessing the Damage as Flooding in Pakistan Moves South
2010-08-27.abstract: Map shows flooding in Pakistan since the initial flash floods that killed hundreds of people in the Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa region.

2010-08-28.title: China’s Growth Leads to Problems Down the Road
2010-08-28.abstract: A nation on a building binge has plans to unclog the highways, but it may take years.

2010-08-28.title: Britain: No Payment for Suicide Bomber’s Widow
2010-08-28.abstract: Two High Court judges ruled against a petition for government payment of legal costs to the widow of one of the four suicide bombers who attacked London’s transit system in 2005.

2010-08-28.title: Kenya: Sudan’s Leader Visits
2010-08-28.abstract: President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan arrived in Kenya on Friday, flouting international demands for his arrest on genocide charges.

2010-08-28.title: Iraq: Zaha Hadid to Design Bank
2010-08-28.abstract: The architect has been appointed to design a new headquarters for the central bank in Baghdad, two months after an assault on the existing building left at least 15 people dead.

2010-08-28.title: Britain: Premier Avoids an Attack
2010-08-28.abstract: British newspapers reported Friday that Prime Minister David Cameron narrowly avoided a Taliban attempt to shoot down his helicopter in Afghanistan.

2010-08-29.title: Upstarts Chip Away at Power of Pakistani Elite
2010-08-29.abstract: Pakistan is urbanizing fast, and powerful forces of change are chipping away at the landed aristocracy.

2010-08-29.title: For Obama, Steep Learning Curve as Chief in War
2010-08-29.abstract: The president has made war decisions after agonizing deliberation but also with dizzying swiftness, while he and the military have had a sometimes edgy relationship.

2010-08-29.title: S. Korea Premier-designate Quits
2010-08-29.abstract: Prime Minister-designate Kim Tae-ho of South Korea resigned on Sunday amid allegations that he lied about his connections with a businessman convicted of bribery.

2010-08-29.title: Afghan Fatalities Rise in Weekend Violence
2010-08-29.abstract: Seven American soldiers were killed in fighting in Afghanistan over the weekend, after several weeks of declining death tolls among NATO forces.

2010-08-29.title: Hamilton Races to Victory Through the Chaos
2010-08-29.abstract: Lewis Hamilton drove a perfect race through wet and dry to win the Belgian Grand Prix and take the lead of the championship.

2010-08-30.title: Nourishing Souls at the Golden Temple
2010-08-30.abstract: Each day at the Golden Temple, tens of thousands of free vegetarian lunches are served by volunteers who embody India’s religious and ethnic mosaic.

2010-08-30.title: A Sikh Temple Where All May Eat, and Pitch In
2010-08-30.abstract: Each day, volunteers at the Golden Temple serve tens of thousands of free lunches.

2010-08-30.title: An Arms Sales Suspect, Bargaining With Secrets
2010-08-30.abstract: Officials believe Viktor Bout might be willing to trade his knowledge of criminal networks for a reduced sentence.

2010-08-30.title: Cricket Scandal Rocks Pakistan
2010-08-30.abstract: For many Pakistanis, weighed down by violence and floods, a betting scandal was the final straw.

2010-08-30.title: Early Obstacle, and Test, at Start of Mideast Talks
2010-08-30.abstract: If the Israeli and Palestinian leaders move beyond the Sept. 26 expiration of Israel’s settlement moratorium, they will demonstrate that this time, they mean business.

2010-08-31.title: Rights Groups Sue U.S. on Effort to Kill Cleric
2010-08-31.abstract: The complicating factor in the case of Anwar al-Awlaki, now hiding in Yemen, is that he is an American citizen.

2010-08-31.title: Petraeus Finishes Rules for Afghan Transition
2010-08-31.abstract: New guidelines for turning some security duties over to Afghan forces call for allied troops to step back from calmer areas.

2010-08-31.title: Germany: Bank Board Member Will Not Quit Over Remarks About Minorities
2010-08-31.abstract: A member of Germany’s central bank board, whose disparaging remarks about Jews and Muslims have created a public outcry, brushed aside calls for his dismissal.

2010-08-31.title: South Africa: President Orders Parties to Settle Public Worker Strike
2010-08-31.abstract: President Jacob Zuma, criticized for going on a state visit to China during a strike by public employees, told government negotiators to return to the bargaining table with unions.

2010-08-31.title: Bahrain: Opposition Leader Charged With Trying to Overthrow Government
2010-08-31.abstract: The Shiite leader Abduljalil al-Singace was arrested in a sweep that the Sunni government described as a counterterrorism operation, but critics call a crackdown on political dissent.
